topic: North Magnetic Pole deviation >18° from 120°E longitude
our_argument_summary: Verdict 'Std Model Explains' — non-discriminating. WMM2025 (NOAA/BGS, Dec 2024) published the NMP at ~139.298°E over 14 months before PROS-006 was registered (2026-03-06); the 18° threshold was already exceeded by 1.298° at registration. WIN-036 is logically downstream of WIN-007 (same NOAA NP.xy pole position, restated as 'deviation from 120°E meridian'). c4 verifies the three minor patches the decider self-applied in suggested-patches-2026-05-06T17-19.json (claim → dome PROS-006 wording; batch members named with both WIN-NNN and dome W-NNN IDs; precision 19.298° + explicit 1.298° margin) all landed correctly in data/wins.json (data/wins.json:WIN-036.detail_claim/detail_evidence/detail_verdict_text).

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: PROS-006 registers a static threshold (>18° east of 120°E meridian); the dome's confirmation transcribes WMM2025's published pole position (139.298°E) and computes 139.298 − 120 = 19.298° exceeding 18° by 1.298°. No live monitoring domain in monitor.py is dedicated to the >18°/120°E test; per c2 and c3 review, Domain #11's lon > 130°E test serves WIN-007 not WIN-036.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Hardcoded transcription. Confirmation value 139.298°E is a static line in raw-text/02-wins.txt:124 ('CONFIRMED (139.298 deg E = +19.3 deg)') and 03-predictions.txt:41. No API call computes this against a live WMM2025 endpoint.
data_sources_used: NOAA NP.xy (manual transcription, no live fetch)
WMM2025 (referenced in raw-text but not fetched)
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: None — the threshold is a binary >18° gate. The '+/-' tolerance pattern present in WIN-038 (Schumann) does not apply here.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None new in c4. c3's tag set (monitoring=hardcoded, relabels_standard=true, post_hoc=true, derives_from_dome=false) remains correct and unchanged by today's patches.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Tag unchanged from c3. WIN-036 (W010) confirmation is a static line in raw-text/02-wins.txt:124 and raw-text/03-predictions.txt:41,67. No monitor.py domain dedicated to the >18°/120°E test. Today's patches do not touch monitoring; the patches edit detail_claim, detail_evidence, and detail_verdict_text only. Tag holds.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Tag unchanged from c3. WMM2025 (NOAA/BGS, Dec 2024) publishes the NMP at ~139.298°E. The 19.298° figure relative to 120°E is the same pole position re-expressed in a different coordinate. Standard geophysics (Livermore et al. 2020, Nat. Geosci., DOI 10.1038/s41561-020-0570-9 — cited via WIN-007 detail_evidence) explains the post-1990 acceleration via flux-lobe dynamics; the dome relabels the cause as 'aetheric-vortex threshold crossing' but predicts no different value than WMM2025. Tag holds. Cross-WIN tag consistency now restored (WIN-007 relabels_standard flipped from false to true via queue_id 305 patch — verified in current data/wins.json).
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Tag unchanged from c3. WMM2025 published 139.298°E in Dec 2024 (raw-text/03-predictions.txt:41); PROS-006 registered 2026-03-06 — over 14 months later. The 18° threshold was already exceeded by 1.298° at registration time (139.298 − 120 = 19.298°). The c3 patch round explicitly added the '1.298° margin already in the bag' framing to detail_verdict_text — strengthens the post-hoc characterization but does not change the tag.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: Tag unchanged from c3. 120°E is a geographic reference meridian; 18° is a chosen threshold. Neither is derived from disc_radius=20,015 km or firmament_height=9,086 km. raw-text/03-predictions.txt:40 frames PROS-006 as 'consistent with the post-1990 exponential trajectory' — i.e., NOAA-data extrapolation, not dome-equation derivation. Tag holds.

holes_found.description: Wording drift introduced by ISS-NEW-WIN036-CLAIM patch: detail_claim now ends '...confirming the post-1990 exponential trajectory.' but the dome's actual PROS-006 register text (raw-text/03-predictions.txt:40) says 'consistent with the post-1990 exponential trajectory.' The c3 recommendation was to match the dome's PROS-006 wording; the patch matched everything except this one verb. 'Confirming' (past-tense indicative, asserting the trajectory has been validated) is a stronger claim than 'consistent with' (subjunctive, hedged). A defender can rebut: 'You said you would mirror our PROS-006 language but you re-asserted the conclusion in your own framing.' Cosmetic but a defender will catch it because the patch rationale explicitly cites this as the goal.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace 'confirming' with 'consistent with' in WIN-036 detail_claim to match raw-text/03-predictions.txt:40 verbatim. One-word patch.

holes_found.description: OTS-canary cross-page inconsistency persists from c3 — NOT addressed by today's patch round. WIN-036 detail_verdict_text still says 'OTS timestamps status_history.json, which bundles prediction parameters together with observation results in a single file.' The Timestamp Error tab (build-scripts/generate-html.js around line 1820) says 'OpenTimestamps anchors status_history.json, the file containing reference data: observed values, pass/fail results, and statistical comparisons. These are observations — the outcomes. The prediction parameters — the formulas, expected values, and tolerances — live in monitor.py source code and docs/model.html.' The same OTS boilerplate appears verbatim in WIN-035, WIN-037, WIN-038, WIN-039 detail_verdict_text. This is tracked in open-issues as ISS-1867 (severity major, status assigned-analyst, found_date 2026-05-06) — flagging here for visibility because the queue_id 304 push only addressed the 3 c3 minors. The major c3 finding remains live; analyst expansion needed before this WIN's narrative is bulletproof.
holes_found.recommendation: Already routed to analyst via ISS-1867. Audit the actual contents of https://john09289.github.io/predictions/api/current/status_history.json and harmonize the language across the 6 OTS-affected WINs (031, 035, 036, 037, 038, 039) and the Timestamp Error tab. Expected resolution: status_history.json contains prediction labels/descriptors + thresholds + results; the dispute is whether 'prediction parameters' in our boilerplate means 'labels' (true) or 'logic/formulas' (false, those are in monitor.py). Pick one consistent characterization site-wide.

holes_found.description: Defense_survives=3 from c3 advocate_mode persists — NOT addressed by today's patch round. Tracked as ISS-1868 (severity moderate, status assigned-analyst). The steel-man defense the dome can mount: (1) the dome's exponential trajectory model was fit to NOAA NP.xy data and committed to the dome repository before WMM2025 was published; (2) the 18° threshold is structurally bound to that trajectory model — not copied from WMM2025; (3) the OTS chain (even granting the Timestamp Error critique) at least proves the threshold parameter existed at registration time as a numerical commitment of the dome's trajectory, not a WMM2025 readout. The c3 patches don't add a sentence preempting this defense. We've added the '1.298° margin already in the bag' but haven't pre-rebutted 'so what — our trajectory committed to the path before you saw the WMM number.' A defender reading the WIN can still mount this without our text engaging it.
holes_found.recommendation: Already routed to analyst via ISS-1868. Add one sentence to detail_evidence or detail_verdict_text noting that both WMM2025 and the dome's trajectory are fits to the same public NOAA NP.xy time series and have not been shown to make distinguishable predictions. Pre-empts the 'we predicted the trajectory, not copied WMM2025' defense.

stronger_arguments.description: Cite the WMM2025 release date precisely. 'Released December 2024' is correct but the WMM2025 5-year epoch officially begins 2025-01-01 (the model is published Dec 2024 to enter service Jan 2025). The pole position 139.298°E is the WMM2025 epoch-start declaration, not a rolling measurement — meaning it is locked in as part of the model's definition. This is stronger than 'WMM2025 published the value 14 months before PROS-006' because it makes clear the value is the model's *constitutive* declaration, not a transient measurement that could have moved.

kernel_of_truth.description: The patches landed cleanly and address real c3 weaknesses. (a) The pre-patch detail_claim 'unprecedented and predictive' was strawmanning — the dome doesn't claim PROS-006 is unprecedented (that's our editorializing); the patch removed it (data/wins.json:WIN-036.detail_claim, current text 'Magnetic pole deviation exceeds 18° east of the 120°E meridian, confirming the post-1990 exponential trajectory.'). (b) Naming the batch members (WIN-035/036/037/038/039 plus dome W009/W010/W011/W013/W015) makes the 'one batch, registered together, all post-hoc' claim auditable rather than vague (data/wins.json:WIN-036.detail_evidence). The dome IDs are verified against raw-text/03-predictions.txt:66-70 — exact match. (c) The 19.298°/1.298° precision is the dome's own confirmation value (raw-text/02-wins.txt:124 and raw-text/03-predictions.txt:41), and the explicit 1.298° margin makes the post-hoc characterization arithmetically sharper.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: These are presentational improvements to a verdict ('Std Model Explains') that was already correct at c3. They do not change the substantive finding — WIN-036 is still a transcription of WMM2025's published pole position, registered 14 months after WMM2025's release, with a threshold already exceeded at registration time (per data/wins.json:WIN-036.detail_evidence and raw-text/03-predictions.txt:41). The major c3 hole (OTS-canary cross-page inconsistency, ISS-1867) and the moderate c3 hole (defense_survives=3, ISS-1868) both persist; the patches addressed the three smaller c3 issues but left the substantive ones for analyst follow-up.

advocate_mode.best_defense: After today's patches, the defender's strongest line is unchanged from c3: 'Your patch round added the 1.298° margin and named the batch members, but you still haven't engaged with the fact that our trajectory model was committed before WMM2025 was published. We didn't read 139.298°E off a NOAA bulletin and write it down — we extrapolated forward from earlier NP.xy data and registered a threshold our model said the trajectory would cross. WMM2025 then validated the trajectory, but our trajectory is what was timestamped, not WMM2025's number.' The c3 minor patches don't touch this defense — they tighten our editorial language but don't pre-empt the substantive counter-claim. ISS-1868 captures this; the analyst expansion is the right path.
advocate_mode.what_we_should_say_instead: Add one sentence: 'Both the dome's trajectory model and WMM2025 are fits to the same publicly available NOAA NP.xy time series; the dome has not demonstrated a numerical prediction distinguishable from WMM2025, and the 19.298° value at threshold-crossing is identical in both.' That converts the 'we predicted the trajectory' defense from a steel-man to an own-goal: yes you predicted the trajectory, but so did WMM2025, from the same data, with no observable difference.

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: ONE outstanding (down from TWO at c3). (1) FIXED: WIN-007 code_analysis.relabels_standard now equals true (verified in current data/wins.json) — flipped via queue_id 305 patch addressing c3 moderate finding. The two pole-position WINs now agree on tagging. (2) PERSISTS: OTS-canary boilerplate in WIN-035/036/037/038/039 still describes status_history.json as bundling prediction parameters with observations, while the Timestamp Error tab describes it as observations-only. ISS-1867 tracks; analyst follow-up pending. The c4 patches do not touch this. The cross-page inconsistency is the c4 round's principal outstanding finding.
cross_win_consistency.actions_recommended: Watch ISS-1867 for analyst expansion progress. The fix is mechanical once analyst audits the live status_history.json contents and picks a consistent characterization.

claims_checked: Batch member dome IDs (W009, W010, W011, W013, W015) match raw-text/03-predictions.txt:66-70 exactly (verified by grep)
Batch member WIN IDs (WIN-035, WIN-036, WIN-037, WIN-038, WIN-039) match dome W-IDs as listed in raw-text/02-wins.txt and raw-text/03-predictions.txt (verified by grep)
Patch landings: PATCH 1 (detail_claim → PROS-006-style wording) verified True; PATCH 2 (batch members named) verified True; PATCH 3 (precision 19.298° + 1.298° margin) verified True
Cross-WIN tag consistency: WIN-007 code_analysis.relabels_standard now True (verified) — c3 moderate finding closed via queue_id 305 patch
quantitative_verification.computations: Arithmetic and string substring checks performed in python against current data/wins.json (post-pull). All 3 minor patches landed cleanly. The new 'confirming' vs 'consistent with' verbal slip is the only quantitative-text discrepancy not previously flagged.

tldr_review.tldr_notes: TLDRs not modified by today's patches. tldr_evidence (current 237 chars) cleanly summarizes 'WMM2025 published the value 14 months before the dome registered the prediction; same trajectory, no independent mechanism.' tldr_verdict (current 132 chars) summarizes the same in shorter form. Both remain factually correct after the c4 patch round.

deeper_analysis:
c4 was triggered by priority-queue item 304 (pushed_at 2026-05-06T17:20:59Z) verifying the decider's self-apply of three minor patches from c3 (detail_claim wording → PROS-006 alignment; batch members named with both WIN-NNN and dome W-NNN IDs; precision 19.298° with explicit 1.298° margin). All three patches landed correctly in data/wins.json — verified by direct substring checks against the post-pull repository state. The c3 moderate finding (cross-WIN tag mismatch on WIN-007 relabels_standard) is also resolved: WIN-007 now reads relabels_standard=true, addressed by the parallel queue_id 305 patch (which is on the queue for separate adversarial review).

What persists from c3:
- MAJOR: OTS-canary cross-page inconsistency between WIN-035/036/037/038/039 detail_verdict_text and the Timestamp Error tab. Tracked as ISS-1867 (severity major, status assigned-analyst). Today's patches do not touch this; analyst expansion is the right path.
- MODERATE: defense_survives=3 — the dome's 'we predicted the trajectory, not copied WMM2025' steel-man defense is unrebutted in WIN-036's prose. Tracked as ISS-1868 (severity moderate, status assigned-analyst). Analyst expansion needed.

New in c4:
- MINOR: 'confirming the post-1990 exponential trajectory' vs dome's actual PROS-006 'consistent with the post-1990 exponential trajectory'. The patch rationale explicitly aimed for PROS-006 alignment but matched every word except this verb. One-word fix; cosmetic but defender-noticeable because the patch's stated goal was wording-matching.

Verdict 'Std Model Explains' continues to hold with high confidence (0.94 ↑ from c3's 0.93). The patches sharpened the post-hoc characterization (the 1.298° margin parenthetical is rhetorically sharp) and made the batch-membership claim auditable (named IDs verified against raw-text). The remaining open work is the c3 major and moderate findings, both already routed to analyst.
